title: M8: CI/CD pipeline + production launch
description:
Ship to production.

CI/CD setup:
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml: Node 20 → npm ci → npm run build → rsync over SSH on push to main
- Optional .github/workflows/staging.yml on push to develop → staging path
- Required GitHub secrets: SSH_HOST, SSH_USER, SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, SSH_TARGET_PATH
- Founder generates SSH keypair, adds public key to host authorized_keys, adds 4 secrets to repo

Server config (.htaccess in dist/):
- Force HTTPS
- Cache headers: immutable max-age=31536000 for /\_astro/* (hashed); no-cache for HTML
- Pretty URLs (already handled by Astro build.format: 'directory')
- Custom 404 → /404.html per locale
- gzip/brotli via mod_deflate if available

Launch checklist:
- DNS cutover plan (TTL lowered 24h before)
- Old URL redirect map (legacy Laravel routes → new equivalents) via .htaccess RewriteRule
- Submit sitemap to Google Search Console + Bing Webmaster
- Verify with Rich Results Test for JSON-LD
- Smoke test: home, both locales, 1 case study, /founder, /contact WhatsApp link, /blog
- Tag git release v1.0.0
- Archive Laravel branch as legacy/laravel-cms (force-push if needed)

Post-launch:
- Monitor 24h for 404s via host access logs
- Confirm rsync deploy round-trip < 2 min on push
acceptance_criteria:
- Push to main triggers GitHub Actions, completes < 5 min, site updates within 2 min
- All legacy URLs either resolve or 301 redirect to new equivalents
- HTTPS enforced, SSL valid
- Lighthouse on production mirror dev numbers
- Search Console verified, sitemap submitted
- Rollback procedure documented and tested once
- v1.0.0 git tag pushed

title: M7: SEO, OG images, performance audit
description:
Final cross-cutting pass before launch.

SEO:
- astro-seo on every page with title + description from frontmatter
- @astrojs/sitemap configured with i18n filter
- robots.txt allow-all + sitemap reference
- hreflang tags on every page (id, en, x-default)
- JSON-LD: Organization (home), Article (blog), BreadcrumbList (detail), CreativeWork (portfolio)
- Canonical URLs

OG images:
- Auto-generated via Satori for every route
- Template: brand mark + title + bucket/category tag
- Twitter card meta added

Performance:
- Image audit: AVIF + WebP fallback, sized variants, blur placeholders, loading='lazy' below the fold
- Font preload on critical pages
- Inline critical CSS (Astro built-in)
- Lighthouse run on / (id + en), /portfolio, /portfolio/reab, /founder, /blog, /blog/[any post]
- Target: mobile >= 95 perf, 100 a11y, 100 best-practices, 100 SEO

A11y:
- axe-core run on every page; zero violations
- Manual keyboard nav + screen reader pass
- Color contrast verified (lime accent on ink only for text)
acceptance_criteria:
- Lighthouse mobile >= 95/100/100/100 on at least 6 sampled routes
- Sitemap includes all pages, both locales
- OG images render correctly when sharing on Twitter/LinkedIn (manual preview)
- axe-core: zero violations site-wide
- All hreflang pairs validated

title: M5: Services + About + Founder pages
description:
Three long-form static pages, all bilingual.

/services:
- Hero with category overview
- 3 category sections (Customer-facing / Internal ops / Product engineering)
- Each lists: tagline, deliverables bullets, ideal-for bullets
- CTA at bottom

/services/[slug]:
- Per-service detail (e.g., Website & Customer Experience, Mobile App Development, Dashboard & Internal Systems, Membership & Loyalty Systems, Product Discovery & MVP Development, Maintenance & Enhancement)
- Optional FAQ section
- CTA

/about:
- Company narrative (per brief 'Draft about page copy')
- 5 message pillars (Business understanding / Technical credibility / Practical execution / Regional roots / Founder-led trust)
- Small testimonial grid (if any)
- CTA

/founder:
- Hero: founder photo (professional), name 'Fajrian Aidil Pratama', title 'Founder & Director'
- Headline 'Led by engineering experience across product, platform, and operational systems'
- 1–2 paragraph narrative
- Selected experience: FounderExperienceStrip with logos for GoTo Financial, Reku, Stockbit/Bibit, Ulearna, public-sector context (no dates, no titles per brief)
- 5 credibility bullets
- Link out: LinkedIn / GitHub / Medium / YouTube
- CTA: 'Schedule a call'

Author employer logos as monochrome SVG in src/assets/companies/.
acceptance_criteria:
- All pages render in both locales with hreflang
- Founder employer logo strip displays 4 logos with hover-tooltip context
- /services and /services/[slug] both work
- Copy sourced from brief draft sections (founder draft, about draft, service cards)
- Lighthouse a11y >= 100 on /founder

title: M4: Portfolio system + 6 case studies
description:
Portfolio index + per-project detail + 6 launch case studies authored bilingually.

Index page (/portfolio, /en/portfolio):
- Bucket filter island (All / Consumer / Public Sector / Operational)
- Card grid sorted by featured + order
- Each card: cover, bucket tag, client, year, summary, hover parallax

Detail page (/portfolio/[slug], /en/portfolio/[slug]):
- Hero cover image
- Metadata strip (client / year / bucket / stack)
- Problem section
- Solution section
- Screenshot gallery wrapped in DeviceFrame components
- Impact bullet list
- External links (Play Store / App Store / web / github)
- Pinned testimonial card if a testimonial references this project
- 'Next case study' link

6 case studies to author (id + en MDX, real screenshots from founder):
- Reab (consumer, Ulearna international, 50k+ downloads)
- Carwa — Digital Car Auction (consumer, Ulearna)
- Caretaker.id (consumer, healthcare)
- Patonro (public-sector, regional tax)
- Lontara (public-sector, regional tax)
- PICO Sulteng (public-sector, COVID-19 platform)

Founder will provide screenshots; until then use anonymized DeviceFrame placeholders.
acceptance_criteria:
- All 6 detail pages render in both locales
- Bucket filter works without page reload (island)
- All images optimized via astro:assets (AVIF + WebP fallback, blur placeholder)
- DeviceFrame component handles phone, tablet, desktop, and blurred (NDA) variants
- Each case study has hreflang alternate links
- Lighthouse on a sample case study mobile >= 95 perf

title: M3: Home page
description:
Build the full home page in both ID and EN. Sections in order:

1. Hero — headline ('Build digital systems that actually help your business grow' / ID equivalent), subheadline, primary + secondary CTA, mock device collage with 3 angled product screens
2. Trust strip — Engineering-led delivery / Cross-context experience / Mobile, web, dashboard / Practical solutions
3. About snippet — partner teknologi narrative
4. Services preview — 3 categories (Customer-facing / Internal ops / Product engineering) with icon + tagline + link
5. Why Banua Coder — 4 differentiators (Business-aware / Engineering-led / Low-risk / Cross-context)
6. Process — 6 steps (Discovery → Solution Mapping → Design & Validation → Development → Testing & Launch → Support & Iteration)
7. Portfolio highlight — 3 featured case studies with bucket tags
8. Founder strip — photo + name + headline + employer logo strip + 'Read more' link
9. Featured testimonial slot (hides if none)
10. Final CTA — 'Konsultasikan Kebutuhan Anda'

Copy is sourced from the brief. Author both id and en MDX in src/content/pages/home.{id,en}.mdx so non-dev edits work.
acceptance_criteria:
- Both / and /en/ render fully populated home with real content
- All sections responsive (320px → 1440px)
- Lighthouse mobile >= 95 perf, 100 a11y, 100 best-practices, 100 SEO
- All links resolve (no 404s in nav)
- Hero loads under 1.8s LCP on slow 4G simulation
- Reduced motion path verified

title: M2: Design system & primitives
description:
Build the core component library and visual primitives. Output is a /\_dev/components review page (excluded from prod build) that renders every component in light + dark, both locales.

Components to ship:
- Layout: Nav, Footer, MobileDrawer (Vue/Svelte island), LangSwitcher (island)
- Typography: SectionHeader (with [ 01 ] bracketed numerals + // mono micro-label), Prose (MDX wrapper)
- Atoms: Button (primary CTA + ghost), Card, Tag, MonoLabel
- Hero: Hero, DotGridBackdrop (parallax, prefers-reduced-motion aware)
- Service: ServiceCard
- Portfolio: CaseStudyCard, BucketFilter (island), DeviceFrame (phone/tablet/desktop variants, blurred variant for NDA)
- Process: ProcessStep
- Founder: FounderCard, FounderExperienceStrip (logo strip with hover context tooltips)
- Trust: TrustStrip, ClientLogoRow (grayscale, color-on-hover)
- Social proof: Testimonial
- CTA: CTA block component
- Theme toggle (island) defaulting to prefers-color-scheme

Setup:
- Geist + Inter + JetBrains Mono via @fontsource, preloaded
- Color tokens locked: --bnc-ink, --bnc-paper, --bnc-accent (#E8FF5A), warm stone scale
- Fluid type scale via clamp()
acceptance_criteria:
- /\_dev/components renders every component in light + dark
- WCAG AA contrast verified on all combos (axe-core or manual)
- prefers-reduced-motion disables parallax + scroll fades
- Mobile drawer opens/closes, traps focus, closes on Esc
- LangSwitcher updates URL correctly
- Lighthouse on /\_dev/components: a11y >= 95
notes:
M2 completed on feature/m2-design-system. 12 commits, 22 components across 9 folders, /dev/components review page (noindex + sitemap-excluded).

Verified:
- pnpm exec astro check: 0 errors
- pnpm run build: green
- Dev page properly noindexed and excluded from sitemap
- WCAG AA, prefers-reduced-motion, dark mode + FOUC prevention all working
- Hand-coded BC monogram SVG (extraction from stamp infeasible due to clip-path complexity)

Known issues for M3:
1. /en/ route does NOT build HTML — agent removed src/pages/en/index.astro thinking i18n fallback would handle it, but Astro static output doesn't generate /en/ redirect HTML automatically. M3 must restore an explicit src/pages/en/index.astro (or pages/[...locale]/index.astro pattern) when building the real home page.
2. Hand-coded icon-only SVG letterforms differ slightly from official wordmark — user should review against PNG1 reference and request adjustments if proportions feel off.
3. Founder photo placeholder still in FounderCard — needs real headshot before M5.
4. Client/company SVG logos still missing — text fallback works but real logos preferred for M3+.

Final M2 git log:

ac2943f feat(m2): add interactive components (lang switcher, theme toggle, bucket filter, device frame)
62f3225 feat(m2): add trust client logo row with text fallback
73a8321 feat(m2): add founder card and experience strip
8afd8a6 feat(m2): add card components (service, case study, testimonial)
0a83bd7 feat(m2): add section components (hero, dot grid, trust strip, cta, process step)
f6e67df feat(m2): add atoms (button, card, tag) and brand logo component
2493cb7 feat(m2): add typography primitives (section header, prose, mono label)
ed72abf feat(m2): add layout primitives (nav, footer, drawer, container)
35b7dcc feat(m2): add baselayout boot script for theme + hreflang
30776c0 feat(m2): extend design tokens with spacing, type scale, and shadows
47c953a feat(m2): extract icon-only svg and update favicon

title: M1: Foundation — Astro 5 scaffold, i18n, content collections, design tokens
description:
Scaffold the new Astro project with everything downstream milestones depend on.

Tasks:
- Archive current Laravel codebase to branch legacy/laravel-cms, push, then nuke source tree on develop
- npm create astro@latest with TypeScript strict
- Install: @astrojs/mdx, @astrojs/sitemap, @astrojs/rss, tailwindcss v4, @tailwindcss/vite, @astrojs/check
- Configure astro.config.mjs: site URL, output 'static', i18n { defaultLocale: 'id', locales: ['id','en'], routing: { prefixDefaultLocale: false }, fallback: { en: 'id' } }
- Tailwind v4 setup with CSS custom properties for tokens (--bnc-ink, --bnc-paper, --bnc-accent, etc.)
- Create src/content/config.ts with Zod schemas for portfolio, services, blog, authors, testimonials, pages collections
- Self-host fonts via @fontsource (Geist, Inter, JetBrains Mono)
- Create src/data/clients.ts and src/data/founder-experience.ts stubs
- Create empty src/assets/{clients,companies}/ for logos
- README with dev/build/deploy commands
acceptance_criteria:
- npm run build completes without errors
- Both / (ID) and /en/ render a placeholder page
- Zod schema validation triggers on intentional bad MDX
- Tailwind v4 CSS tokens accessible in components
- Type-check (astro check) passes
notes:
M1 completed on feature/m1-foundation.

Stack landed: Astro 6.3.1 + Tailwind v4.2.4 + pnpm 10.26 + Node 22.16. MDX + sitemap + RSS + zod schemas (6 collections) + i18n (id default, en fallback) + self-hosted fonts (Geist/Inter/JetBrains Mono) + design tokens (--bnc-ink/paper/accent + warm stone scale).

Verification: pnpm exec astro check = 0 errors. pnpm run build = green, both /index.html and /en/index.html generated. Zod validation confirmed via deliberate bad MDX test. No package-lock.json.

Deviations from plan: (1) fonts loaded via CSS @import in global.css instead of JS imports in BaseLayout — cleaner Tailwind v4 idiom; (2) zod import path changed to 'astro/zod' (was 'astro:content') — Astro 6 API change.

Open follow-ups for M2:
- Real SVG logos for src/assets/{clients,companies}/
- Confirm accent color #E8FF5A (lime) before visual review starts
- Upgrade lucide-astro → @lucide/astro (deprecation warning)
- Investigate harmless /en route conflict warning under trailingSlash:'always' i18n

6 commits on this branch; ready for PR to develop after M1 acceptance:
0e75767 chore(m1): remove legacy laravel codebase
6b84fd4 feat(m1): scaffold astro 6 with mdx, sitemap, and tailwind v4
6e02a45 feat(m1): add design tokens, base layout, and bilingual placeholder pages
e1c1a05 feat(m1): add zod schemas and content collection scaffolding
78432d6 feat(m1): add static data stubs and asset dirs
3d89121 docs(m1): replace default readme and finalize gitignore

title: Banua Coder website redesign
description:
Replace the legacy Laravel 8 + Blade CMS template with a modern, static, content-as-code Astro 5 site that positions Banua Coder as an engineering-led technology partner per the brief at docs/banua_coder_company_profile_website_brief.md.

Stack: Astro 5 (static) + Tailwind v4 + MDX content collections + bilingual i18n (ID/EN). Deploy via GitHub Actions + rsync over SSH to the existing shared host. WhatsApp deeplink for contact (no form backend). No data migration from the legacy Laravel CMS.

Full design: docs/plans/2026-05-08-banua-coder-redesign-design.md
acceptance_criteria:
- Live site replaces the current banuacoder.com
- Both ID and EN locales render with hreflang
- All 6 launch case studies (Reab, Carwa, Caretaker.id, Patonro, Lontara, PICO Sulteng) published bilingually with screenshots
- Services index + per-service detail pages
- Founder page with employer logo strip (GoTo Financial, Reku, Stockbit/Bibit, Ulearna)
- Blog scaffolded with at least 1 launch post
- Lighthouse mobile >= 95 on home + 1 case study
- WCAG AA passes
- GitHub Actions deploy on push to main works end-to-end
- Legacy Laravel codebase archived to legacy/laravel-cms branch

title: Banua Coder back-office (app.banuacoder.com)
description:
Separate Laravel 12 app at app.banuacoder.com for internal business operations. Refrens-style: clients, quotations, invoices, payments (Xendit + Lemon Squeezy), contracts with inline editing + e-sign, plus Indonesian accounting for PPH Badan / PPN / PPH 23 monthly summaries.

Architectural decision: separate app from the Astro marketing site at banuacoder.com. Marketing stays static + CDN-friendly; back-office gets full server runtime, DB, payment webhooks, PDF generation.

Stack proposal:
- Laravel 12 + Filament v3 (admin UI; CRUD-heavy + auth out-of-the-box) OR Laravel + Inertia + Vue 3
- PostgreSQL or MySQL
- Spatie Browsershot for PDF (invoices, quotations, contracts)
- Tiptap rich-text editor for contract bodies (versioning + variables)
- DocuSeal for e-sign OR custom click-to-sign with audit log + IP/timestamp
- Xendit for IDR payments (Indonesia-first), Lemon Squeezy for USD international (MoR)
- Postmark / Mailgun / SES for transactional email
- Tax: PPH 23 (2% jasa), PPN (11%), PPH 21 monthly summaries, exportable for accountant

Sequencing: starts AFTER marketing site M3-M8 ships.
acceptance_criteria:
- Live at app.banuacoder.com with HTTPS
- Auth: founder + future team logins
- Client CRUD with contact log
- Quotation create/send/accept/convert-to-invoice
- Invoice + payment via Xendit (IDR) and Lemon Squeezy (USD)
- Contract create with rich-text editor + public sign link + audit log
- Monthly accounting summary (income/expense/PPH 23/PPN) exportable as CSV/PDF for accountant
- Indonesian + English UI and PDF templates
- Backup strategy in place
- Founder onboarded and using the system

title: M6: Blog + Testimonials + Contact
description:
Three independent surfaces.

Blog:
- /blog index — card grid paginated 12 per page, sorted by publishedAt desc
- /blog/[slug] — article layout (Prose component) with author bio strip at end
- /rss.xml feed
- Tag filter island (optional, can defer)
- 1 launch post drafted in MDX (announcement + positioning piece)

Testimonials:
- src/content/testimonials/ collection wired
- Featured testimonial rotator on home (above final CTA, hides if empty)
- Pinned testimonial card on portfolio detail when project reference matches
- Small testimonial grid section on /about
- Empty-state-friendly: ship with zero, add later via MDX edit only

Contact:
- /contact and /en/contact
- Headline + intro per brief
- Primary CTA: WhatsApp deeplink (wa.me/[number]) with prefilled message:
  'Halo Banua Coder, saya tertarik untuk konsultasi mengenai [project type].'
- Secondary: email (mailto:), Instagram, LinkedIn, GitHub
- Office address + simple embedded map (or static map image to avoid Google Maps JS)
- Hours of operation
acceptance_criteria:
- /blog renders with at least 1 launch post
- RSS feed validates
- Testimonials collection empty by default; site builds clean
- WhatsApp deeplink prefills correctly on mobile + desktop
- Map renders without external JS (or async-load only)
- All bilingual
